heading: Update Snapshot Copy Policy
body:
To update the snapshot copy policy for your cluster using the
Atlas UI:

If Copy to other regions is enabled, this section
displays a table of your currently configured snapshot copy
policies.

Toggle Automatically Sync Copy Regions with Cluster
Regions to On or Off to enable or disable
automatic distribution of snapshot copies to all secondary regions
in a multi-region cluster.

When you enable Automatically Sync Copy Regions with
Cluster Regions, Atlas configures snapshot copy policy
items for each secondary region in your cluster and keeps the
policy in sync with the cluster configuration as you add or remove
secondary regions. Atlas creates each snapshot copy policy
item with the same frequency and retention times as the backup
policy items defined for the cluster.

> **Important:** When you enable Automatically Sync Copy Regions
> with Cluster Regions, Atlas overwrites any existing
> snapshot copy policy items you have configured.

Make your changes to the snapshot copy policy items in the table.

To add a new snapshot copies policy item, click Add
Another Region and Frequency and configure the new item in the
new row.

> **Note:** If Automatically Sync Copy Regions with Cluster Regions
> is set to On, you can't add or delete copy regions,
> but you can modify the frequency and retention times for the snapshot
> copy policy items that Atlas automatically configures for each
> secondary region in your cluster.

A dialog box appears, summarizing your new snapshot copies policy
configuration. If you made changes, you can select checkboxes for
the following options to apply your changes to existing snapshots
in the copy regions:

- Update retention time of existing snapshot copies

- Delete all snapshots from removed policies

> **Note:** Updates made with this method only apply to snapshot copies
> created after February 11, 2026. To update snapshot copies
> created before this date, you must use the `updateSnapshots`
> option in a `PATCH` request to the Atlas Administration API
> [/backup/schedule](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2)
> endpoint.
